rationale: Reuses an existing bulk command that already emits confidence_score/outcome_confidence/formatted under the exact key names sample's Python reads, with zero code changes, no new CLI surface, and no subprocess fan-out. Scored 12/12 vs Option A's 10/12 (bulk CLI flag) and Option B's 3/12 (per-issue subprocess loop).
rule: Option C — switch sample state to ll-issues refine-status --json
alternatives_rejected: Option A (add --include-scores flag to list --json): well-precedented but adds unnecessary CLI surface area and duplicated frontmatter-read logic. Option B (per-issue show --json loop): no existing perf-gate precedent, incomplete (show --json has no formatted field), would need ~2N subprocess calls.
